description.p: Northstar is a clean-room web browser written from scratch in C, with a GTK 4 user interface and a libcurl network stack. It is built to be small, secure, and readable by a single person end to end.

ul.li: A from-scratch HTML5, CSS, and JavaScript engine — no forked browser engine.
One window, one page, one process: the page engine runs in the shell process behind a seccomp + Landlock syscall sandbox on Linux.
No JIT, which keeps the JavaScript attack surface small.
No telemetry: it does not phone home and does not track the user.

description.p: Email client, media, and rendering release.

ul.li: Built-in email (about:email): IMAP/POP3/SMTP with one-step provider setup for Gmail, Yahoo, iCloud and Fastmail, app-password and 2-step-verification guidance, and attachment viewing.
An optional primary password encrypts saved mail credentials at rest with PBKDF2-SHA256 and AES-256-GCM.
Media: initial Media Source Extensions (MSE) append path.
Rendering: replaced elements (img, video, canvas, svg) are sized per the CSS rendering spec and default to inline-level.
Networking: per-host concurrent-fetch cap and tighter media/renderer IPC bounds.

description.p: Extensions, content blocking, and engine hardening release.

ul.li: Initial WebExtensions support with declarativeNetRequest content blocking and EasyList/ABP filter lists.
Local SafeBrowsing-style phishing and malware protection.
Updated vendored QuickJS and Lexbor engines, with DOM, XHR, and CSS spec-compliance fixes.
Shared curl multi handle with HTTP/2 connection multiplexing and reuse.
Tabbed Developer Tools window expanding the JavaScript console.
Net Center masthead refresh and sign-in button layout fixes.
Inline atomic box layout positioning correction.
New audio path: SDL2 output with in-tree pl_mpeg (MP2) and minimp3 (MP3) decoding, replacing the vendored miniaudio backend.
Security hardening: bounded EventSource reconnect delay, a fixed image-source memory leak, and a tightened renderer IPC reply limit.
